messages.meaning: Hydraulic system pressure below normal operating range. Either the electric pump has failed or hydraulic fluid has been lost.
messages.immediate: HYD HAND PUMP — OPERATE (30–40 strokes to 1500 psi). If pressure rises: pump has failed — check/pull HYD OIL PUMP CB. Maintain 1500 psi with flaps extended. If pressure does not rise after 30–40 strokes: fluid loss suspected — prepare for flapless landing.

messages.meaning: HYD OIL PUMP circuit breaker is open. Electric pump is not powered. Operating flaps, brakes, or steering will deplete accumulator pressure rapidly.
messages.immediate: Do not reset CB in flight. Use hand pump if pressure required. Plan accordingly for approach and landing.
